title: Decouple Bureau statements from ambient checkout state
goal: Keep Bureau statements available through a deployment-bound, hash-verified, read-only Registry snapshot even when the conventional Bureau checkout is stale or dirty, while requiring every Registry mutation to bind an explicit clean current task worktree.

acceptance.assertion: Every installed Bureau release creates an immutable tracked-file Registry snapshot whose inventory binds source commit, path set, inventory SHA-256 and complete tree SHA-256.

The canonical launcher selects that snapshot by manifest-bound environment instead of the caller's working directory, so statements remain valid when ~/repos/bureau is stale, dirty or used by another process.
Commands explicitly classified as statements use a query-only StateStore path that performs no directory creation, migration, journal-mode change or chmod effect.

A mutating command against the canonical snapshot fails with explicit-registry-root-required and can proceed only when --root names a clean Bureau task worktree whose head matches the deployed source commit.
Missing, malformed, symlinked, source-mismatched or content-drifted snapshots fail closed before Registry truth or mutation authority is claimed.
No path automatically pulls, resets, stashes, cleans, overwrites or removes the conventional main checkout or any foreign worktree.

Focused and full tests cover root precedence, dirty unrelated cwd, read-only SQLite, explicit clean override, blocked implicit mutation and snapshot tampering; live proof repeats the original stale-dirty-main scenario after deployment.

observation.failure: The immutable runtime correctly refused the stale dirty checkout, but because the launcher defaulted Registry root to the caller cwd it also refused every normal statement.

validation.review_correction: The first full-suite pass found that query-only state was applied too broadly to explicit development roots. The implementation was corrected so strict query-only access applies only to canonical-runtime-default; the final full suite then passed.

verified_acceptance.canonical-read-snapshot: Verified live: manifest, inventory and tracked-tree hashes matched the immutable deployment at merge commit 0f556f2dc1a02db40646a82f2ce7dc40d6cc5621.
verified_acceptance.ambient-independence: Verified from /home/alex/repos/bureau at head b65d1f8 with the two foreign dirty Registry files still present and the checkout 94 commits behind: runtime-identity and live-list succeeded through canonical-runtime-default.
verified_acceptance.query-only-statements: Verified by dedicated filesystem and SQLite tests plus a live operational live-list read through the canonical snapshot.
verified_acceptance.explicit-write-worktree: Verified live: close-ready without --root returned explicit-registry-root-required, while runtime identity against the clean exact-merge deployment worktree reported compatible and mutation_allowed=true.
verified_acceptance.tamper-and-drift: Verified by manifest, inventory, symlink and tracked-tree negative tests; drift fails closed.
verified_acceptance.foreign-work-preservation: Verified after merge and deployment: the conventional checkout remained on b65d1f8 with exactly the same two foreign modified files.
verified_acceptance.tests-and-live-proof: Verified by full pytest, focused regressions, Ruff, both GitHub CI matrix jobs and the replay of the original stale-dirty-main scenario.

metadata.verification_note: PR #527 is merged, its exact merge commit is deployed, the original stale-dirty-main failure is live-replayed successfully for statements, implicit mutation remains blocked, explicit clean-worktree compatibility remains available, and foreign changes remain untouched.
metadata.safety_boundary: Availability is restored only for read truth. Mutation authority remains stricter than before and always requires an explicit clean task worktree.
